message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_matrix_norm autograd via primitives + numpy conformance [bd-i4rd]

Surfaced during a /reality-check pass scanning for severed-autograd
sites: tensor_matrix_norm extracted tensor_values, computed the
norm in plain f64, and rebuilt a requires_grad=false leaf — same
severed-autograd pattern fixed under frankentorch-1tax for the
cov / corrcoef / mode / quantile family. PyTorch's
torch.linalg.matrix_norm IS differentiable.

Also corrects the c36b closure note that wrongly claimed
matrix_norm 'has no separate API in ft-api' — it does
(tensor_matrix_norm at lib.rs:11602), it just wasn't covered by
a subprocess conformance harness.

Compose through autograd primitives:
  - 'fro'  = sqrt(sum(A*A))                 via tensor_mul + tensor_sum + tensor_sqrt
  - '1' / '-1'   = max/min(sum_dim(|A|, 0)) via tensor_abs + tensor_sum_dim(0) + tensor_max_dim/min_dim
  - 'inf' / '-inf' = max/min(sum_dim(|A|, 1)) via tensor_abs + tensor_sum_dim(1) + tensor_max_dim/min_dim
Output shape preserved as [1] via tensor_unsqueeze when the final
reduction yields a 0-D scalar.

Adds two regression tests in ft-api:
  - matrix_norm_frobenius_propagates_gradient: A=[[1,2],[3,4]] under
    sum loss, asserts grad = A / ||A||_F = [1,2,3,4]/√30 element-wise.
  - matrix_norm_inf_propagates_gradient_to_max_row: max-row routing
    convention — only the row achieving the max gets non-zero grad
    (sign-of-A on that row).

Adds torch_matrix_norm_numpy_subprocess_conformance covering all
five orderings across 12 matrices (identity, scaled, mixed-sign,
rectangular, 1x1 scalar). Subprocess conformance count: 32.

ft-api lib tests: 1139 passing (+2 from 1137).

Closes frankentorch-i4rd.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankentorch-35e","title":"Update FEATURE_PARITY.md to honestly track unimplemented PyTorch subsystems","description":"FEATURE_PARITY.md currently marks 'Full PyTorch drop-in surface: parity_green' but omits major PyTorch subsystems from the tracking matrix entirely, creating a misleading picture.

What to add:
1. New section: 'Deferred Subsystems (not yet in scope)' with explicit not_started status for: GPU/CUDA backend, Distributed training (DDP/FSDP), JIT/TorchScript/torch.compile, Quantization, Sparse tensors, Mixed precision (AMP), ONNX export, Gradient checkpointing, Custom autograd Functions (torch.autograd.Function).
2. For each: document why it's deferred (e.g., 'CPU-first vertical slice, GPU planned for Phase 3+'), link to any future closure beads.
3. Change 'Full PyTorch drop-in surface: parity_green' to accurately reflect that it covers the CPU eager-mode subset only.
4. Add a 'Parity Scope Declaration' section that explicitly states what IS and IS NOT in scope for the current phase.

This is the single most important documentation fix — without it, the project appears done when it's at ~70% of PyTorch's core API surface.

Tests: N/A (documentation only). Verify by reading the updated file and confirming every major PyTorch subsystem is either tracked as green or explicitly listed as deferred.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:28.495902714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:04:43.553923709Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:04:43.553642487Z","close_reason":"Already completed by another agent: Parity Scope Declaration section added, Full PyTorch drop-in changed to parity_gap, Deferred Subsystems section with 9 entries and tracking beads, Gap Policy updated. Verified all referenced beads exist and tensor_apply_function claim is accurate.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-3cp","title":"Capture committed perf-SLO baseline artifact for G5","description":"Follow-up to frankentorch-vws.\n\nThe perf-SLO runner/gate now exists in ft-conformance, strict cargo gates pass, and we captured a verified single-sample full-profile measurement in artifacts/phase2c/performance/perf_slo_report_full_once.json plus PERF_SLO_SUMMARY_FULL_ONCE.md. The current result shows all five direct spec budgets failing and the three regression budgets remaining baseline_required without a committed baseline.\n\nRemaining work:\n1. Run the full perf-SLO profile with the intended statistical envelope (default 100 iterations, warmups as configured) on a suitable worker/host.\n2. Commit a stable baseline artifact at the canonical perf_slo baseline path for regression comparisons.\n3. Decide whether the single-sample full_once artifact should stay as an exploratory snapshot or be superseded by the committed baseline bundle.\n4. Hand the baseline path/contract to frankentorch-ioo so G5 can call run_perf_slos + check_perf_slos deterministically in CI.\n\nNotes:\n- rch exec validates runtime execution but does not automatically persist generated artifacts back into the local worktree across separate invocations, so baseline capture should use a workflow that intentionally preserves the produced artifact bundle.\n- Direct spec-budget failures from the single-sample full profile are already documented in artifacts/phase2c/performance/PERF_SLO_SUMMARY_FULL_ONCE.md.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T20:53:37.780725984Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-08T03:27:16.468792093Z","closed_at":"2026-04-08T03:27:16.468370434Z","close_reason":"Normalized the canonical perf-SLO measurement/baseline paths, committed perf_slo_baseline_v1.json plus PERF_SLO_BASELINE_V1.md, and aligned runner/checker defaults so G5 has a stable baseline contract. Higher-iteration replacement baseline tracked separately.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-3u6","title":"Audit dispatch/kernel CPU edge cases","description":"Deep audit pass for ft-dispatch/ft-kernel-cpu execution flow; fix bugs/edge cases and add tests as needed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-11T03:24:33.376039097Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T03:44:53.435483764Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T03:44:53.435310637Z","close_reason":"Fixed missing f32 test import; validated checks/tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"frankentorch-3v6e","title":"Remaining ft-api ops with fail-loud autograd guards: stft, istft, affine_grid, grid_sample, complex/view_as_real/view_as_complex","description":"After closing frankentorch-1tax (statistical/special ops), 7 ops in ft-api still reject requires_grad=true inputs with hard errors instead of carrying gradients:

- stft (line 1248) — short-time Fourier transform; PyTorch's torch.stft supports backward via inverse FFT.
- istft (line 1411) — inverse short-time Fourier transform; symmetric to stft.
- stft window tensor (line 6733) — separate guard rejecting tracked window inputs.
- affine_grid (line 7774) — torch.nn.functional.affine_grid supports backward.
- grid_sample (line 7905) — torch.nn.functional.grid_sample supports backward.
- complex (line 994/1001) — torch.complex(re, im) supports backward through real and imaginary inputs (Wirtinger derivatives).
- view_as_real / view_as_complex (lines 1101, 1145) — complex view ops also support gradients in PyTorch.

These are not 'severed-autograd' — they're explicit guards that fail-loud, so users can't accidentally lose gradients silently. But they DO block training paths that would work in PyTorch (e.g., audio models using STFT, vision models using affine_grid + grid_sample for spatial transformer networks).

Each child needs its own fix bead. Children grouped by domain:
  Audio:   stft + istft + stft-window
  Vision:  affine_grid + grid_sample
  Complex: complex + view_as_real + view_as_complex (deferred — Wirtinger calculus is non-trivial; PyTorch's complex autograd took years to stabilize)

Priority for audio/vision (P2): real downstream training paths blocked. Priority for complex (P3): Wirtinger calculus is genuinely tricky and may need its own design doc first.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-29T22:21:35.728631229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T22:21:35.728631229Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-3v6e","title":"Remaining ft-api ops with fail-loud autograd guards: stft, istft, affine_grid, grid_sample, complex/view_as_real/view_as_complex","description":"After closing frankentorch-1tax (statistical/special ops), 7 ops in ft-api still reject requires_grad=true inputs with hard errors instead of carrying gradients:

- stft (line 1248) — short-time Fourier transform; PyTorch's torch.stft supports backward via inverse FFT.
- istft (line 1411) — inverse short-time Fourier transform; symmetric to stft.
- stft window tensor (line 6733) — separate guard rejecting tracked window inputs.
- affine_grid (line 7774) — torch.nn.functional.affine_grid supports backward.
- grid_sample (line 7905) — torch.nn.functional.grid_sample supports backward.
- complex (line 994/1001) — torch.complex(re, im) supports backward through real and imaginary inputs (Wirtinger derivatives).
- view_as_real / view_as_complex (lines 1101, 1145) — complex view ops also support gradients in PyTorch.

These are not 'severed-autograd' — they're explicit guards that fail-loud, so users can't accidentally lose gradients silently. But they DO block training paths that would work in PyTorch (e.g., audio models using STFT, vision models using affine_grid + grid_sample for spatial transformer networks).

Each child needs its own fix bead. Children grouped by domain:
  Audio:   stft + istft + stft-window
  Vision:  affine_grid + grid_sample
  Complex: complex + view_as_real + view_as_complex (deferred — Wirtinger calculus is non-trivial; PyTorch's complex autograd took years to stabilize)

Priority for audio/vision (P2): real downstream training paths blocked. Priority for complex (P3): Wirtinger calculus is genuinely tricky and may need its own design doc first.","notes":"affine_grid slice closed in 669df96 (frankentorch-bfj5). Remaining children that still need work:
  - stft / istft / stft window — Audio. Backward via inverse FFT or windowed-overlap-add gradient. Non-trivial.
  - grid_sample (bilinear mode) — Vision. Backward via bilinear interpolation derivatives in both input and grid; nearest mode is non-differentiable in grid.
  - complex / view_as_real / view_as_complex — Wirtinger calculus. PyTorch's complex autograd took years to stabilize; needs a design doc first.

Each remaining child should get its own bead when picked up. Priorities:
  - stft + istft + stft-window: P2 (audio training paths blocked)
  - grid_sample: P2 (vision training paths blocked, e.g. spatial transformer networks)
  - complex/Wirtinger: P3 (defer until design doc)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-29T22:21:35.728631229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T22:34:02.617918638Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankentorch-40i","title":"Fix f32 index_select/gather/scatter output dtype","description":"Audit: index_select/gather/scatter in ft-autograd always emit F64 tensors even when input dtype is F32, despite f32 CPU kernels existing. This silently upcasts and breaks dtype preservation. Fix by dispatching to f32 kernels and emitting F32 storage, add f32 forward tests in ft-api.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T12:18:13.533424703Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T12:25:26.015235117Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T12:25:26.014918859Z","close_reason":"Implemented f32 index_select/gather/scatter dispatch + tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

PyTorch signature: torch.bincount(input, weights=None, minlength=0)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-27T05:43:08.617216568Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T05:48:53.006005299Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T05:48:53.005724498Z","close_reason":"Implemented tensor_bincount with weights, minlength, edge cases. 6 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-hlf","title":"Deterministic checkpoint canonicalization with duplicate node IDs","description":"normalize_entries should be order-invariant even when duplicate node_id entries exist; sort with deterministic tie-breakers and add coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T19:33:52.392147592Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T19:38:32.230117651Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T19:38:32.229898516Z","close_reason":"Deterministic checkpoint ordering for duplicate node IDs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-hpg","title":"Quantized storage dtypes (QInt8/QUInt8) and QuantizedLinear","description":"Follow-up to frankentorch-4ak. The MVP fake-quantization slice landed in 4f6fff6 covers post-training calibration + QAT-style fake quantize using composed round/clamp ops, which keeps storage in the input dtype.

Remaining V1 work to fully match torch.quantization:
1. Add QInt8 / QUInt8 storage dtypes in ft-core (TensorStorage variant + DType enum + serde paths).
2. Add QuantizedTensorMeta carrying scale + zero_point as part of TensorMeta.
3. Add a QuantizedLinear module in ft-nn that stores INT8 weights and dequantizes on the fly (or invokes a fused INT8 matmul kernel when added).
4. Per-channel quantization (per-axis scale/zero_point vectors).
5. Conformance fixtures comparing against torch.quantization output on a couple of canonical layers.

Out of scope here: GPU INT8 kernels (tracked under frankentorch-c3d).","status":"deferred","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-28T02:17:59.714118325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T03:34:51.991877615Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-i4rd","title":"tensor_matrix_norm has severed-autograd + lacks numpy subprocess conformance","description":"tensor_matrix_norm exists in ft-api/src/lib.rs:11602 supporting fro / 1 / -1 / inf / -inf orderings. It extracts tensor values and rebuilds a requires_grad=false leaf, silently severing the autograd tape (same severed-autograd pattern as the cov / corrcoef / mode / quantile family that was fixed under frankentorch-1tax). PyTorch's torch.linalg.matrix_norm IS differentiable — for 'fro' it's just sqrt of sum of squares (composable through autograd primitives), and for the operator norms it's a max/min over column/row L1 sums (also composable through tensor_abs + tensor_sum_dim + tensor_max/min).

Also missed during the c36b closure: my earlier closure note claimed matrix_norm 'has no separate API in ft-api' — that was wrong, it does. It just wasn't covered by a subprocess conformance harness.

Two fixes needed:
  1. Compose tensor_matrix_norm through autograd primitives (or wrap in tensor_apply_function) so gradients flow.
  2. Add torch_matrix_norm_numpy_subprocess_conformance harness covering all five orderings.

Surfaced during a /reality-check pass scanning for severed-autograd patterns.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Composed tensor_matrix_norm through autograd primitives: 'fro' = sqrt(sum(A*A)) via tensor_mul + tensor_sum + tensor_sqrt; '1'/'-1'/'inf'/'-inf' = max/min over (sum_dim of tensor_abs) via tensor_abs + tensor_sum_dim + tensor_max_dim/min_dim. Output shape preserved as [1] via tensor_unsqueeze when reductions yield 0-D scalars. Added matrix_norm_frobenius_propagates_gradient and matrix_norm_inf_propagates_gradient_to_max_row regression tests in ft-api (1139 passing). Added torch_matrix_norm_numpy_subprocess_conformance covering all five orderings (fro / 1 / -1 / inf / -inf) across 12 matrices. Subprocess conformance count now 32 (was 31).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T17:58:10.617093432Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T18:03:32.411939240Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T18:03:32.408959957Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-i7v","title":"Restore ft-optim rustfmt compliance","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:32:23.707770910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T03:34:06.440692399Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T03:34:06.440430122Z","close_reason":"Completed: restored ft-optim rustfmt compliance","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-iey","title":"Add ONNX model export support","description":"ONNX export allows FrankenTorch models to be deployed in any ONNX-compatible runtime (ONNX Runtime, TensorRT, CoreML, etc.). This is important for production deployment and interoperability.

Implementation plan:
1. Create a new crate ft-onnx (or module in ft-serialize) that:
   a. Traces a model forward pass to capture the computation graph
   b. Maps FrankenTorch ops to ONNX operator set (opset 17+)
   c. Serializes the graph + weights to ONNX protobuf format
2. Add session.export_onnx(model, dummy_input, path) to ft-api
3. Map core ops: Linear, Conv2d, ReLU, BatchNorm, LSTM, Softmax, MatMul, Add, etc.
4. Use the onnx-pb crate for protobuf serialization (or manual protobuf encoding)

Tests:
- Unit test: export a Linear model, verify ONNX file is valid protobuf
- Unit test: exported model contains correct op types and shapes
- Integration test (if onnxruntime-rs available): run exported model and compare outputs
- Edge case: export model with dynamic batch size","status":"deferred","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:19:10.592448782Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T03:34:51.698138970Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankentorch-iey","depends_on_id":"frankentorch-2er","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:19:22.990453482Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankentorch-igu","title":"Add adversarial/fuzz test coverage for high-risk paths (spec Gate G4)","description":"COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENTORCH_V1.md §5 requires 'adversarial fixtures and fuzz/property suites for high-risk entry points' and §18 defines Gate G4 (adversarial + property tests) as release-blocking.

Current state: ft-serialize has 5 proptest properties. ft-core has none. ft-api, ft-autograd, ft-kernel-cpu have none. The spec's Security Threat Matrix (§16) defines 8 threat categories that need adversarial coverage.

Implementation plan:
1. Add proptest properties for ft-autograd:
   - Random computation graphs maintain gradient correctness (numerical gradient check)
   - Backward on disconnected subgraphs doesn't corrupt other gradients
   - In-place mutation version tracking prevents stale gradient reads
2. Add proptest properties for ft-kernel-cpu:
   - Broadcasting produces correct shapes for random input shapes
   - Reduction ops produce correct results for random input tensors (cross-check against naive impl)
   - Matmul associativity: (A*B)*C matches A*(B*C) within tolerance
3. Add proptest properties for ft-api:
   - Random tensor ops maintain shape consistency (output shape matches PyTorch rules)
   - save_state_dict -> load_state_dict round-trip preserves all values for random state dicts
4. Add adversarial fixture tests for ft-serialize:
   - Fuzzed JSON payloads don't cause panics (catch_unwind + verify Error returned)
   - Fuzzed binary payloads don't cause panics
   - Oversized shape dimensions don't cause OOM (our recent checked_mul fix)
5. Add adversarial tests for ft-nn:
   - NaN/Inf inputs to forward() produce NaN/Inf output (not panic)
   - Zero-sized batch inputs handled gracefully

Tests: The beads ARE the tests. Each property test should use proptest with 256+ cases.
Success criterion: cargo test --workspace --all-features passes with all new property tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:18:58.165045573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:24:26.092380322Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:24:26.092126241Z","close_reason":"Added 8 property-based tests: 4 in ft-autograd (add/mul gradient correctness, backward determinism, relu gradient indicator) + 4 in ft-kernel-cpu (neg self-inverse, exp/log round-trip, sum vs naive, softmax sums-to-one). All pass with proptest 256+ cases. Added proptest dev-dependency to ft-kernel-cpu.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
